title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Footpath a Fundamental Right, Calls for Legislation
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on demarcated footpaths is a fundamental right of citizens. The court observed that encroachments and obstructions on footpaths violate citizens' basic rights, particularly the right to life under Article 21. The bench called upon the legislature to enact a specific law to protect pedestrians' right to use footpaths. The ruling has significant implications for urban local bodies and their responsibility to maintain public infrastructure.
exam_angle: Connects to the expanding interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life) by the Supreme Court; relevant for questions on fundamental rights, judicial activism, and urban governance.
key_facts: Right declared: Right to walk on footpaths = Fundamental Right
Constitutional basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Court: Supreme Court of India
Direction: Called for enactment of a specific law protecting pedestrians

title: High Court Upholds India's Temporary Blocking of Telegram, Rejects Platform's Challenge
summary: An Indian High Court has rejected Telegram's petition challenging the government's temporary blocking of its application in India. The court ruled that the government's action of temporarily blocking Telegram was not disproportionate and fell within the scope of executive powers. The blocking was reportedly linked to concerns over criminal misuse of the platform. This ruling reinforces the government's authority under the Information Technology Act to issue blocking orders.
exam_angle: Relevant to IT Act Section 69A (blocking of online content), intermediary liability, and the balance between free speech (Article 19) and reasonable restrictions; also links to data governance and cybersecurity policy.
key_facts: Platform challenged: Telegram
Legal provision involved: IT Act Section 69A (blocking powers)
Court verdict: Government's temporary block not disproportionate
Basis of block: Criminal/security misuse concerns

title: Reliance Jio Board Approves IPO; DRHP to Be Filed with SEBI on June 19
summary: The board of Reliance Jio Infocomm has approved its Initial Public Offering (IPO), which is expected to be India's largest-ever share sale. Mukesh Ambani announced that the Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP) will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) on June 19, 2025. If successful, the Jio IPO would surpass the LIC IPO (2022) as the biggest IPO in Indian history. The listing is anticipated to significantly impact Indian capital markets and the telecom sector.
exam_angle: Relevant to capital markets, SEBI's regulatory role, IPO process, and India's telecom sector; also connects to topics on corporate governance and FDI/domestic investment in infrastructure.
key_facts: Company: Reliance Jio Infocomm (subsidiary of Reliance Industries)
DRHP filing date: June 19, 2025, with SEBI
Expected to be India's largest-ever IPO (surpassing LIC IPO of 2022)
Regulator: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)

title: El Niño Poses Potential Threat to India's Growth and Inflation Outlook
summary: Analysts and economists have flagged El Niño as a significant upcoming challenge for the Indian economy, with potential impacts on agricultural output, food inflation, and GDP growth. El Niño weather patterns typically suppress monsoon rainfall in India, adversely affecting kharif crop production. Any shortfall in monsoon precipitation can trigger food price inflation, stressing rural incomes and RBI's monetary policy decisions. India's dependence on the monsoon for over 50% of its agricultural water needs makes El Niño a macro-economic risk factor.
exam_angle: Links El Niño (climate phenomenon) to India's agricultural economy, food inflation, and RBI monetary policy; important intersection of GS Paper 1 (Geography/Climate) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Agriculture).
key_facts: El Niño: Warming of central/eastern Pacific Ocean surface temperatures
Impact on India: Suppresses southwest monsoon, reduces kharif output
Economic risk: Food inflation, pressure on RBI rate decisions
Over 50% of India's agriculture depends on monsoon rainfall

title: Amazon Highlights Water Conservation Measures in India Amid Data Centre Environmental Scrutiny
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has pointed to its water conservation initiatives in India as scrutiny over the environmental impact of data centres intensifies globally. The company highlighted steps taken to reduce water consumption in its Indian data centre operations, including water recycling and efficiency measures. Data centres are significant consumers of water for cooling systems, raising sustainability concerns as India expands its digital infrastructure. The issue is relevant to India's broader policy on sustainable technology infrastructure and green computing.
exam_angle: Connects to environmental sustainability of digital infrastructure, water resource management, and India's data centre policy; relevant for GS Paper 3 (Environment and Economy) and essay questions on technology vs. sustainability.
key_facts: Company: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
Issue: Water consumption by data centres for cooling
India's response: AWS highlighting water recycling and conservation steps
Context: Global scrutiny on environmental footprint of AI/cloud data centres

title: Indian IT Sector Stocks Slump up to 7% Following Accenture's Revenue Outlook Cut
summary: Indian IT stocks witnessed a sharp decline of up to 7%, triggering a broader market sell-off that wiped out approximately Rs 1.3 lakh crore in market capitalisation. The sell-off was triggered by Accenture's decision to cut its revenue growth outlook, which spooked investors about discretionary IT spending by global clients. The Sensex closed around 600 points lower while the Nifty remained above 24,000. The event highlights India's IT sector's sensitivity to global technology spending trends, particularly from the US and Europe.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's IT/services export sector, stock market indices (Sensex/Nifty), and the impact of global macroeconomic signals on domestic capital markets; useful for GS Paper 3 economy questions.
key_facts: Trigger: Accenture cut its revenue growth outlook
Market impact: Indian IT stocks fell up to 7%; Rs 1.3 lakh crore market cap eroded
Indices: Sensex fell ~600 points; Nifty held above 24,000
Sector affected: Indian IT/technology services exporters

title: Trump Names PM Modi and Xi Jinping as Most Admired World Leaders
summary: US President Donald Trump, when asked which world leader he admires most, named Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Chinese President Xi Jinping. This statement is significant given the current geopolitical context of US-China trade tensions and the evolving US-India strategic partnership. Trump's praise for Modi reflects the continued importance of the India-US bilateral relationship under his administration. The comment comes amid active diplomatic engagements between India and the US on trade, defence, and technology.
exam_angle: Relevant to India-US bilateral relations, India's global diplomatic standing, and the geopolitics of the Indo-Pacific; useful for GS Paper 2 (International Relations) and essay questions on India's foreign policy.
key_facts: Statement by: US President Donald Trump
Leaders named: PM Narendra Modi (India) and Xi Jinping (China)
Context: Ongoing US-India strategic partnership and US-China trade tensions
Significance: Reflects India's rising global diplomatic influence

title: Trump Administration's Iran Nuclear Deal Negotiations: Strategic and Diplomatic Implications
summary: The Trump administration's ongoing nuclear deal negotiations with Iran have significant strategic implications, with commentators noting that the success or failure of any agreement rests heavily on Iran's response. A potential deal would involve curbing Iran's nuclear programme in exchange for sanctions relief. The outcome has implications for global oil markets, Middle East stability, and India's energy security given India's historical reliance on Iranian crude oil. India has been a key buyer of Iranian oil before US sanctions compelled a halt.
exam_angle: Relevant to India-Iran-US trilateral relations, India's energy security, and global non-proliferation frameworks (NPT, IAEA); important for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Energy Security).
key_facts: Parties: USA and Iran (nuclear deal negotiations)
Core issue: Iran's nuclear programme vs. sanctions relief
India angle: India was a major buyer of Iranian crude before 2018 US sanctions
Geopolitical impact: Middle East stability, global oil prices
